industry.valueProp: Boutique mid-market RIA specializing in outdoor industry founders — a niche HNW/UHNW client base with concentrated equity and liquidity-event complexity

recentSignals.signal: Hired first dedicated compliance lead in October
recentSignals.implication: Firm is formalizing compliance infrastructure for the first time — classic trigger for workflow tooling purchases; new compliance hire will immediately feel the pain of manual processes and will want to own a tool decision early.

recentSignals.signal: Posted three open operations roles last week
recentSignals.implication: Rapid ops headcount growth signals scaling pains; a larger ops team makes per-seat workflow software easier to justify and more necessary.

recentSignals.signal: Announced expansion to Denver and Salt Lake City offices
recentSignals.implication: Multi-office RIAs face coordination and audit-trail complexity that single-office firms don't — compliance workflow software becomes structurally more valuable.

culturalFit.evidence: Niche focus on outdoor industry founders signals identity-driven firm-building, not AUM-at-all-costs growth. Boutique positioning and founder-led structure suggest high-trust, relationship-first culture. 'Trailhead' branding reinforces deliberate, values-laden identity. Outreach should feel like a peer conversation, not a vendor pitch.

tierRationale: Three concurrent signals — first compliance hire, multi-office expansion, and ops team buildout — converge exactly on the problem our software solves for SEC-registered RIAs in the 50-500 household range. The compliance lead is almost certainly in 30-day onboarding and forming opinions on tooling right now. Window is narrow and hot.
outreachAngle: Address the newly hired compliance lead (or Marcus DePaul as gatekeeper) directly: "Trailhead just brought on its first compliance lead the same month you announced two new office locations — that's a tough combination to land in on day one. We work with several single-compliance-officer RIAs that went multi-office and built their SEC exam readiness workflows in our platform before their first cross-office review. Worth a 20-minute conversation before the Q1 audit prep cycle kicks off?"
